summary: Early surfactant administration within 30–60 minutes after birth reduces the risk of chronic lung disease and neonatal mortality compared to delayed use. Timely intervention minimizes pulmonary damage from mechanical ventilation and high oxygen levels, leading to better long-term respiratory outcomes for preterm babies.
